title: Coelogyne parvula
intro: Coelogyne parvula, also known as the small Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and protection against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne parvula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Coelogyne parvula is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the subtribe Coelogyneinae, which is a subtribe of the tribe Coelogyneae within the orchid family. The tribe Coelogyneae is a group of orchid tribes within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This species of orchid is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in shaded forests. Coelogyne parvula is typically classified as a terrestrial orchid.
